title: Pressers, Textile, Garment, and Related Materials
description: Press or shape articles by hand or machine.

tasks.text: Hang, fold, package, and tag finished articles for delivery to customers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate steam, hydraulic, or other pressing machines to remove wrinkles from garments and flatwork items, or to shape, form, or patch articles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Straighten, smooth, or shape materials to prepare them for pressing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove finished pieces from pressing machines and hang or stack them for cooling, or forward them for additional processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Finish pleated garments, determining sizes of pleats from evidence of old pleats or from work orders, using machine presses or hand irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lower irons, rams, or pressing heads of machines into position over material to be pressed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify and treat spots on garments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Shrink, stretch, or block articles by hand to conform to original measurements, using forms, blocks, and steam.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Finish fancy garments such as evening gowns and costumes, using hand irons to produce high quality finishes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Push and pull irons over surfaces of articles to smooth or shape them.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Finish pants, jackets, shirts, skirts and other dry-cleaned and laundered articles, using hand irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Slide material back and forth over heated, metal, ball-shaped forms to smooth and press portions of garments that cannot be satisfactorily pressed with flat pressers or hand irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select appropriate pressing machines, based on garment properties such as heat tolerance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Spray water over fabric to soften fibers when not using steam irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position materials such as cloth garments, felt, or straw on tables, dies, or feeding mechanisms of pressing machines, or on ironing boards or work tables.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Moisten materials to soften and smooth them.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean and maintain pressing machines, using cleaning solutions and lubricants.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Press ties on small pressing machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Block or shape knitted garments after cleaning.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Activate and adjust machine controls to regulate temperature and pressure of rollers, ironing shoes, or plates, according to specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Use covering cloths to prevent equipment from damaging delicate fabrics.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine and measure finished articles to verify conformance to standards, using measuring devices such as tape measures and micrometers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Finish velvet garments by steaming them on bucks of hot-head presses or steam tables, and brushing pile (nap) with handbrushes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure fabric to specifications, cut uneven edges with shears, fold material, and press it with an iron to form a heading.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert heated metal forms into ties and touch up rough places with hand irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Brush materials made of suede, leather, or felt to remove spots or to raise and smooth naps.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sew ends of new material to leaders or to ends of material in pressing machines, using sewing machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select, install, and adjust machine components, including pressing forms, rollers, and guides, using hoists and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
